qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: C2 worktree-recognition surface (9 symbols + 1 early-allow branch) fully removed from /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py. _evaluate_commit deny-funnel restored to single blessed-path posture: extract msg → AC-A12 blessed-bridge → AC-A2 inline-env block → AC-A13 default-deny → grant find/validate/unlink. The 'enterprise commit posture = ONE blessed path' invariant is satisfied: no second un-audited path exists.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Smoke harness contains intentional 'post-C2-rollback' / 'C2-rollback' historical-framing references in NEW comments per AC4 reattribution intent. These are NOT orphan code refs (no deleted symbols are mentioned), only narrative prose. Pre-existing harness coherence wart from prior cycle (k2-k9 live under `if want k;` so --filter k5 cannot isolate k5) is also documented in BA ticket's Out-of-Scope Observations.

qa.overall_assessment: C2 rollback is complete, clean, and structurally correct. All 8 ACs PASS by direct verification (grep / ast.parse / diff -u / diff -q / sha256 comparison / live smoke harness execution / live external substring harness execution / BA ticket inspection). 4 always-on safety layers preserved with correct line citations and unique substring grep targets. 10/10 smoke matrix rows PASS (9 deny rows + bulk-detector regression + 1 blessed-bridge allow). Zero critical/major/minor code findings; 2 evidence-wording caveats from codex adversarial review classified as in_scope_minor and addressed below.

AC1.evidence: Verified by Grep tool: pattern `\bC2\b|_C2_|C2_BULK_SHAPE_RE|_worktree_recognition_allows_commit|_cwd_in_worktree_list|_sentinel_matches_session|_wtline_matches` against /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py returns ZERO matches. Legitimate `work-tree` substring preserved at line 99 inside GIT_GLOBAL_OPTION_RE alternation `r'--(?:git-dir|work-tree|namespace|exec-path|super-prefix|config-env)'` — this is the git pre-option redirect blocker for AC-A2/AC-A13, NOT a C2 symbol. (Codex evidence-wording caveat: a bash literal `grep -- '--work-tree'` would not match because `--` is separate from `work-tree` in the regex alternation; the AC1 intent — preserving legitimate git pre-option handling — is functionally satisfied; AC1 grep target should be `work-tree` substring, not literal `--work-tree`.)

AC2.evidence: Read /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:526-550. _evaluate_commit body executes in order: (1) msg = _extract_commit_message(command) [:528]; (2) BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE.search(msg) early-allow with _observe_bridge_commit [:529-534]; (3) _inline_env_present(command, 'CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE') → _block_inline_env_commit AC-A2 [:535-537]; (4) os.environ.get('CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE') != '1' → _block_default_deny_commit AC-A13 [:538-540]; (5) _find_grant [:543-545]; (6) _validate_commit_grant_message + _validate_commit_grant_files [:547-548]; (7) _unlink_grant [:550]. No branch precedes blessed-bridge check. No C2 early-allow branch present.

AC4.evidence: diff -u /var/tmp/baseline-20260511-094500/commit-smoke.sh.pre /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/commit-smoke.sh confirms: (a) header comment lines 1-48 reattributed away from 'C2-recognized worktree' framing toward 'bulk-detector regression (no C2 dependency)' and 'AC-A2 inline-env block + AC-A13 default-deny'; (b) k2-k9 note_pass/note_fail messages reattributed to AC-A13 (k2/k3/k4/k5/k6/k7/k9) or AC-A2 (k8); (c) k-ordinary subtest inserted inside `if want k;` after k9 (the SOLE permitted net-add) with payload `git commit -m "feat: ordinary"`, runs under env -u CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE to strip ambient env, asserts rc=2 AND stderr contains 'only the blessed /merge' (AC-A13 substring); (d) ALL k2-k9 JSON test PAYLOADS byte-identical to baseline — diff -u grep for `"tool_name"|"command":` lines returns ONLY the new k-ordinary payload as `+`, zero `-` payload lines.

AC5.evidence: Baseline snapshot at /var/tmp/baseline-20260511-094500/ contains 7 .pre files + SHA256SUMS.pre (verified by ls). Per-pair awk-extraction + live-repo sha256sum comparison: ALL 5 surviving-cycle files byte-identical to baseline. diff -q reports 'files are identical' for: commands/commit.md (35da1a11...), hooks/commit.sh (c521daf0...), hooks/lib/close-verdict.py (bab87b06...), hooks/pretool-bulk-commit-detector.py (f523a992...), scripts/check-security-hook-drift.sh (90b8cc85...). privilege-guard.py diff is DELETION-ONLY (zero `+` lines, 51 `-` lines removing C2 helpers and early-allow). commit-smoke.sh diff shows: comment/message reattribution (paired +/- in C2-prose regions) + one contiguous `+` block for k-ordinary subtest.
AC5.quote: diff -q + sha256sum match across 5 surviving-cycle files; diff -u privilege-guard.py shows 0 added, 51 removed (deletion-only)

AC6.evidence: Independently executed dev's /tmp/ac6-verify.py harness. All 10 rows PASS with explicit rc + substring assertion: (a) k2 bulk-shape → rc=2 + 'agent git commit - only the blessed /merge' (AC-A13); (b) k3 git -C redirect → rc=2 + AC-A13 substring; (c) k4 -F file → rc=2 + AC-A13 substring; (d) k5 --no-pager -C → rc=2 + AC-A13 substring; (e) k6 -c pager=false -C → rc=2 + AC-A13 substring; (f) k7 decoy -m +-F → rc=2 + AC-A13 substring; (g) k8 inline-env injection → rc=2 + 'agent git commit - inline-env injection blocked' (AC-A2); (h) k9 -C worktree from outside → rc=2 + AC-A13 substring; (i) k-ordinary plain commit → rc=2 + AC-A13 substring (the rollback's positive proof); (j) AC-A12-positive 'auto-bulk: end-of-cycle commit for master' → rc=0 (blessed-bridge allow). Separately ran `bash /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/commit-smoke.sh --filter k` → 10/10 PASS reported by the harness itself.
AC6.quote: ac6-verify.py output: 10/10 PASS with substring assertions; commit-smoke.sh --filter k: pass=10 fail=0 skipped=0

AC7.evidence: All 4 always-on safety layers verified preserved: (1) AC-A2 inline-env block — _evaluate_commit calls _inline_env_present + _block_inline_env_commit at hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:536-537; deny-message function _block_inline_env_commit at :379-390; substring 'agent git commit - inline-env injection blocked' unique at :382 (push-side at :568 has 'git push' not 'git commit', so commit-path token disambiguates per BA iter-1 codex finding #1). (2) AC-A12 blessed-bridge allow — _evaluate_commit calls BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE.search(msg) + _observe_bridge_commit at :529-534; regex `BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE = re.compile(r'auto-bulk:\s*end-of-cycle commit for\b')` at :95 (anchored, not over-permissive). (3) AC-A13 default-deny — _evaluate_commit checks os.environ.get('CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE') != '1' + _block_default_deny_commit at :538-540; deny-message function _block_default_deny_commit at :392-407; substring 'agent git commit - only the blessed /merge' unique at :395 (no push counterpart). (4) Slash-command frontmatter — commands/commit.md:3 reads exactly 'disable-model-invocation: true' (verified by Grep tool); byte-identical to baseline via AC5 surviving-cycle diff-q identity check.

AC8.evidence: Read /root/docs/dev/ticket-20260511-094500.md:256-264 'Deferred Follow-up — C2-v2 (NOT in this cycle)'. Section is directional and capability-based: (a) 'mediation MUST rely on a capability token that the privilege-guard can verify as proof that the commit was issued by an audited wrapper'; (b) explicit failed-attempt layer 'L3 (data — regex on argv shape) + L4 (logic — cwd/sentinel match)' with rationale 'argv-shape regex was version-fragile'; (c) explicit future-target layer 'L5 (infrastructure — process-level capability propagated by an audited wrapper)'; (d) 'NOT in scope of the future cycle's spec until separately authorized': concrete env-var names, hook code, regex hardening, smoke test shapes, acceptance criteria. The note is FORECAST, not a concrete deliverable in this cycle.

evidence_wording_caveats.description: Dev report's 'orphan-reference sweep returns ZERO matches' is precise only for the exact deleted-symbol pattern (_wtline_matches | _cwd_in_worktree_list | _sentinel_matches_session | _worktree_recognition_allows_commit | C2_BULK_SHAPE_RE | _C2_REDIRECT_RE | _C2_MSG_FILE_RE | _C2_MSG_INLINE_RE). Generic 'C2' / 'post-C2-rollback' references remain in scripts/commit-smoke.sh as INTENTIONAL historical-framing prose per AC4 reattribution intent. These are not orphan code refs.
evidence_wording_caveats.action: Documented here for /close debate clarity; no code change needed. Dev report wording is technically accurate for deleted-symbol orphans; QA report adds this precision to head off ambiguity.

evidence_wording_caveats.description: BA ticket AC1 specifies the legitimate-preservation grep target as `--work-tree`. Functionally the substring `work-tree` IS present at line 99 inside the GIT_GLOBAL_OPTION_RE alternation `r'--(?:git-dir|work-tree|namespace|exec-path|super-prefix|config-env)'`. However the literal token `--work-tree` is NOT a contiguous substring in the file (the `--` prefix is OUTSIDE the alternation in the regex). A bash `grep -- '--work-tree'` would return no match. The AC1 INTENT — preserving legitimate git pre-option handling — is satisfied; the verification mechanism should target the `work-tree` substring inside GIT_GLOBAL_OPTION_RE, not the literal `--work-tree` token.
evidence_wording_caveats.action: Documented for /close debate. Functional rollback is correct; this is a wording precision issue, not a code defect.

codex_consult.transcript_excerpt: Verdict: PASS on the rollback logic, with evidence caveats. Substantive points: (1) Deny-funnel order is clean post-deletion. (2) k-ordinary is a valid positive rollback proof. (3) AC-A2/AC-A12/AC-A13 safety layers are preserved. (4) Draft QA has an overclaim on orphan sweep — tighten wording before close. (5) AC1 literal `--work-tree` evidence is slightly wrong — should say `work-tree` substring in GIT_GLOBAL_OPTION_RE. (6) Environment caveat (codex used /root/.claude.bak copy because /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude not accessible from codex sandbox). No blocking code bug found.
codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, reasoning=xhigh) returned PASS on rollback logic with 2 evidence-wording caveats (orphan-sweep wording precision + AC1 grep-target wording) and 1 environment caveat. No blocking code bugs.

Finding #4 (orphan-sweep wording) classified in_scope_minor — documented as EWC-1 in QA report; dev's wording is accurate for deleted-symbol orphans, QA adds precision for /close clarity. Finding #5 (AC1 --work-tree literal) classified in_scope_minor — documented as EWC-2; functional preservation is intact, only the verification-target wording needs tightening. Finding #6 (codex sandbox couldn't access /dev/shm/) is out_of_scope for code verification — I DID verify directly on /dev/shm during this QA session.
